introduction: In Karnataka, marriage registration is mandated by the Karnataka Marriages (Registration and Miscellaneous Provisions) Act, 1976. The Kaveri 2.0 portal facilitates an efficient, technology-driven application process for acquiring the legal certificate.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Processing Time: Typically same day after physical verification with the Sub-Registrar.
  • Application Fee: Base fee of ₹10 (plus administrative Kaveri portal charges around ₹50-₹100).
  • A Marriage Certificate is valid for a lifetime.
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Marriage Registration

Note: You need to provide documents for Proof of Age, Proof of Address, Photographs, and Witnesses.
Identity & Age Proof
  • SSLC Certificate
  • Passport
  • PAN Card
Proof of Address
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Passport
  • Recent Utility Bills
Marriage Proof & Affidavit
  • Marriage invitation card
  • Receipt from a marriage hall (Kalyana Mantapa)
  • Joint photographs in wedding dress
  • Joint/individual certified affidavit stating marital status
Witnesses
  • Three witnesses
  • Witness Aadhaar cards / ID proofs
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via Kaveri 2.0 Portal

Karnataka's Kaveri 2.0 portal handles all registration services including marriage registration with online pre-entry and appointment booking.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit Kaveri 2.0 Portal
    Go to kaveri.karnataka.gov.in. This is the official Registration, Stamps & Fiscal Policy Department portal of Karnataka.
  2. Register / Login
    Click "Sign Up". Enter your name, mobile number, and email. Verify via OTP. Log in with your credentials.
  3. Select Pre-Registration → Marriage Registration
    From the dashboard, click Pre-Registration → "Marriage Registration". Select the applicable Act: Karnataka Marriages Act or Hindu Marriage Act.
  4. Fill All Details
    Enter: groom's and bride's full names (as per SSLC/Aadhaar), date and place of marriage, Kalyana Mantapa receipt number, and full details of 3 witnesses including their Aadhaar numbers.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned JPG/PDF copies (max 1 MB each): SSLC Certificate, Aadhaar, marriage hall receipt, affidavit on e-Stamp paper, joint wedding photo, and 3 witness ID proofs.
  6. Pay Fee Online & Book Appointment
    Pay \u20b910 base fee + portal charges (~\u20b950–\u20b9100) via payment gateway. Select your Sub-Registrar Office and book an available appointment slot.
  7. Print Acknowledgment
    Print the appointment slip and application summary. Carry it along with all originals to the SRO on the appointment date.
Download Certificate: After SRO registration, the digitally signed certificate is available to download from Kaveri 2.0.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Bapuji Seva Kendra / Grama One / Sub-Registrar

Visit a Bapuji Seva Kendra, Grama One Centre, or the local Sub-Registrar Office to submit a physical application. Working hours: Monday – Friday, 10:00 AM – 5:30 PM.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Locate Your Centre
    Find the nearest Bapuji Seva Kendra or Sub-Registrar Office. Use the office locator on Kaveri 2.0. The marriage should have taken place within that sub-district's jurisdiction.
  2. Collect Application Form
    Request the Marriage Registration Application form from the counter. It is available free of cost.
  3. Fill Form in BLOCK LETTERS
    Enter: names (as per SSLC/Aadhaar), marriage date & place, religion, names and addresses of 3 witnesses, and residential addresses of both spouses.
  4. Prepare Notarized Affidavit
    Get a joint affidavit on \u20b9100 e-Stamp paper, signed by both spouses, sworn before a Notary Public. This is mandatory under the Karnataka Marriages Act.
  5. Attach Self-Attested Copies
    Attach: SSLC Certificate, Aadhaar, marriage hall receipt (Kalyana Mantapa), wedding invitation, joint wedding photo, affidavit, and ID proofs of 3 witnesses. Bring originals.
  6. Submit, Sign & Pay
    Submit the form at the counter. The groom, bride, and all witnesses sign the register in front of the Sub-Registrar. Pay \u20b910 + nominal charges at the counter.
  7. Collect Certificate
    The Marriage Certificate is issued same day after signing. A digitally signed copy can also be downloaded from kaveri.karnataka.gov.in later.
Helpline: Karnataka Registration Dept helpline: 080-22203758. For Kaveri portal issues contact support.kaveri@karnataka.gov.in.
afterApplying:
After Registration - What Next?

Physical Verification

The bride, groom, and the three witnesses must be present at the Sub-Registrar's office on the scheduled date.

Certificate Generation

Original documents will be physically inspected. Both parties will sign the central marriage register. A digitally signed or physically stamped certificate is issued.
